# lattice work-namespace schema

## Summary
The `lattice-wiki` vault collapses the previous `issues/` + `roadmap/` split into a single `<vault>/work/` namespace. Discrimination is by `kind:` (8 values), lifecycle is unified across all kinds (7 states), and the committed plan lives in the body as a `## Plan` markdown table (not in frontmatter). A sibling `<vault>/work-index.json` sidecar gives consumers (notably [[wiki/plugins/lattice-workflows/lattice-workflows]]) a single denormalized read instead of crawling markdown.

Captured in adrs/0008-unified-work-namespace; full design in 2026-05-lattice-ecosystem-schema-refinements §2.4.

## Frontmatter shape

```yaml
---
title: <Title>
category: work
kind: bug                       # required — 8-value enum
summary: <one-line>
status: open                    # required — 7-state lifecycle
severity: medium                # bug | security | perf — optional otherwise
effort: small                   # trivial | small | medium | large
blast_radius: package           # file | package | domain | system
affects:
  - packages/<pkg>
target: 2026-Q2                 # feature | initiative — optional otherwise
owner: pat                      # populated when in-progress
opened: 2026-04-21
updated: 2026-05-03
related_tickets: []
related_prs: []
resolved_in: ""                 # required when resolved
superseded_by: ""               # required when superseded
mitigation: ""                  # required when mitigated
rationale: ""                   # required when wontfix
tags: [<topic>, <area>]
---
```

## The eight `kind` values

| Kind | Origin | Typical shape |
|---|---|---|
| `bug` | discovered | symptom + root cause + fix |
| `tech-debt` | discovered | suboptimal pattern + refactor target |
| `test-gap` | discovered | missing coverage + test plan |
| `security` | discovered | exposure + remediation |
| `perf` | discovered or measured | regression + budget + fix |
| `feature` | intended | user-driven capability + scope |
| `initiative` | intended | multi-feature effort spanning weeks/quarters |
| `spike` | intended | time-boxed exploration with a question |

`data-model-defect` and `doc-drift` fold to *tags*, not separate kinds — same lifecycle, same required fields as `bug` and `tech-debt` respectively.

## The seven-state lifecycle

| State | Meaning | Required fields |
|---|---|---|
| `open` | filed; no committed plan | — |
| `accepted` | plan committed; `## Plan` table populated; ready to start | `## Plan` non-empty |
| `in-progress` | someone is implementing | `pr` or `branch` |
| `mitigated` | symptom hidden, root cause persists (mostly bug/security/perf) | `mitigation` |
| `resolved` | done | `resolved_in` |
| `wontfix` | closed without action | `rationale` |
| `superseded` | replaced by another work item | `superseded_by` |

==`investigating` is not a state.== Live data from existing vaults shows nobody used it. The "stuck-open" lint rule (default 30-day threshold) replaces "stuck-investigating" with the same effect.

Transitions are mostly forward; `accepted → open` is allowed when a plan is invalidated by new evidence.

## Per-kind field applicability

| Field | Required for | Allowed for | Disallowed for |
|---|---|---|---|
| `severity` | none (triage step) | `bug`, `security`, `perf`, `tech-debt`, `test-gap` | `feature`, `initiative`, `spike` |
| `target` | none | all kinds — meaningful for `feature`, `initiative` | — |
| `owner` | none | all kinds — populated when `in-progress` | — |
| `effort` | none | all kinds | — |
| `blast_radius` | none | all kinds | — |

## Effort time-anchors

`trivial` = minutes (one-line change), `small` = hours (single file + tests + single PR), `medium` = days (multi-file, possibly cross-package), `large` = weeks (multiple PRs, possibly an initiative). Anchors are advisory.

## Blast_radius rule

`file | package | domain | system`. ==Practical impact, not source-code locality.== A one-line change to a heavily-used shared package can be `system` even though the source is in one package. Same for shared CDK constructs and cross-domain DB schemas.

## The sidecar — `<vault>/work-index.json`

Committed, denormalized, regenerated by [[wiki/plugins/lattice-work/lattice-work]] (`regenerate_work_index.py`). Schema_version 1. Carries `generated_at`, `vault_commit`, counts (by status / kind / severity / blast_radius), and per-item digest with `slug`, `title`, `kind`, `status`, `severity`, `effort`, `blast_radius`, `affects`, `target`, `owner`, `plan_steps`, `opened`, `updated`, `superseded_by`, `tags`.

**Freshness contract**: any item's `updated:` newer than `generated_at` ⇒ sidecar stale ⇒ regenerate or warn. Lint flags as `sidecar-stale`.

## Where the lint lives

- **In `lattice-wiki`**: base structural lint — frontmatter parses, `category:` is in the enum, required base fields present, staleness, duplicate titles. Walks `<workspace>/` (per [[wiki/sources/2026-05-workspace-relative-wikilinks-linter-and-content-rewrite]]) so `work/**` pages are reachable; pages carry an `is_work` flag that ==exempts work items from orphan detection== (work items legitimately have no inbound wiki backlinks). Excludes `<vault>/work/archived/` from its walks. Per the schema-vs-tooling split — see adrs/0004-work-tracker-as-consumer-plugin.
- **In `lattice-work`**: ==19 rules== in the `work_layer` group covering enum validity, state-conditional required-field presence, `affects[]` resolution (filesystem-only in v1 per inline ADR-W3 in 2026-05-work-tracker-v1-design), plan-table parsing, stuck-state heuristics, sidecar freshness, and archive eligibility. The wiki page previously rounded to 17; the v1 design spec's actual table gives 18, plus the `archive-eligible` rule = 19. See [[wiki/plugins/lattice-work/lattice-work]] for the full rule table.

## The `<vault>/work/archived/` sub-namespace

Terminal-status items (`status ∈ {resolved, wontfix, superseded}`) move into `<vault>/work/archived/` on demand via `/lattice-work:archive`. Per 2026-05-archive-resolved-work-items:

- ==`archived/` is a location, not a state.== Items keep their `resolved` / `wontfix` / `superseded` frontmatter; ==no 8th lifecycle status is added.== Mirrors the package-layer precedent at `<vault>/packages/archived/<name>/`.
- Items there ==retain the full schema== — same frontmatter, same `## Plan` body convention, same wiki page semantics, same Obsidian openability.
- Base structural lint (`lattice-wiki`) and the work-tracker sidecar regen ==exclude this sub-path from their walks==. Archived items therefore drop out of `<vault>/work-index.json` and don't show up in `/lattice-workflows:next` or `:status`.
- `mitigated` is ==not eligible== for archive — it's partial and can flip back to `in-progress`.
- Eligibility is measured against `updated:`, not a new `closed_at:` field. Reusing `updated:` avoids a schema change rippling through code-wiki and workflows; the failure mode (typo-fix on a resolved item resets the clock) is rare and recoverable via `--min-age-days 0` or targeted-mode archive with the explicit slug.
- ==No automated validation== that items in `archived/` still have terminal status — a manual `git mv` of a non-terminal item into `archived/` won't be caught. Remedy: `git mv` it back. Acceptable trade-off for v1.
- Restore in v1 is a manual recipe: `git mv <vault>/work/archived/<slug>.md <vault>/work/<slug>.md && /lattice-work:regen-index`. A `:restore` command is the obvious v1.1 if this becomes friction.
